findings.title: CLAUDE.md appears stale or too vague
findings.description: CLAUDE.md exists but may not contain enough stable project memory.
findings.recommendation: Refresh CLAUDE.md with current setup commands, product boundaries, and test expectations.

findings.title: OpenAPI file not detected
findings.description: No OpenAPI schema was found. This may be fine for projects without an HTTP API.
evidence: No openapi.* or swagger.* file was scanned.
findings.recommendation: For API products, publish an OpenAPI spec with operation descriptions and examples.

findings.title: MCP-related files or packages detected
findings.description: MCP signals were found and can be evaluated by future deeper analyzers.
findings.recommendation: Ensure each MCP tool has a clear name, description, input schema, and safety guidance.

findings.title: Command verification probes were skipped
findings.description: Static analysis is default; project commands are not executed unless explicitly enabled.
evidence: Run with --probe commands or --run-probes to execute safe script probes.
findings.recommendation: Use command probes in trusted local or CI environments when you want executable verification.
findings.agentFailureMode: Without command probes, AgentLighthouse can tell agents what commands appear to exist, but not whether they currently pass.

AGENTS.md.contentPreview:
# Agent Instructions

## Project Overview

This project demonstrates clear MCP tool metadata.

## Setup Commands

```bash
npm test
```

```bash
npm run lint
```

```bash
npm run typecheck
```

## Architecture Map

- `src/mcp-server.example.txt`: MCP tool registrations.
- `agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml`: benchmark workflows.

## Coding Conventions

Tool names must be action-resource specific and descriptions must explain when not to use the tool.

## Common Mistakes

- Do not use generic tool names

# Claude Memory

## Workflow

# MCP Good Project

## Quickstart

```bash
npm test
```

## Example

The server exposes `search_public_docs` for read-only documentation search and `revoke_sandbox_token` for explicitly approved sandbox token cleanup.

## Architecture

- `src/mcp-server.example.txt` contains MCP tool definitions.
- `agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml` contains verifiable agent workflows.
- `AGENTS.md` contains agent operating instructions.

## Troubleshooting

If auth fails, use an `EXAMPLE_TOKEN` placeholder and never

textByPath.AGENTS.md:
# Agent Instructions

## Project Overview

This project demonstrates clear MCP tool metadata.

## Setup Commands

```bash
npm test
```

```bash
npm run lint
```

```bash
npm run typecheck
```

## Architecture Map

- `src/mcp-server.example.txt`: MCP tool registrations.
- `agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml`: benchmark workflows.

## Coding Conventions

Tool names must be action-resource specific and descriptions must explain when not to use the tool.

## Common Mistakes

- Do not use generic tool names like `run`.
- Do not edit generated build output.
- Do not omit privacy notes for tools that touch user data.

## Security and Privacy

Never expose secrets. Tools that access private data must state auth and privacy constraints.

## Ownership

Maintainers review any tool that writes, revokes, deletes, or accesses private data.

textByPath.README.md:
# MCP Good Project

## Quickstart

```bash
npm test
```

## Example

The server exposes `search_public_docs` for read-only documentation search and `revoke_sandbox_token` for explicitly approved sandbox token cleanup.

## Architecture

- `src/mcp-server.example.txt` contains MCP tool definitions.
- `agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml` contains verifiable agent workflows.
- `AGENTS.md` contains agent operating instructions.

## Troubleshooting

If auth fails, use an `EXAMPLE_TOKEN` placeholder and never paste real secrets into prompts.
textByPath.agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml:
version: 0.1.0
project: mcp-good-project
tasks:
  - id: add-readonly-tool-example
    title: Add a read-only MCP tool example
    persona: MCP server maintainer
    objective: Add an example invocation for search_public_docs that uses safe placeholder input and documents the output shape.
    projectAreas:
      - src/mcp-server.example.txt
      - README.md
    requiredDocs:
      - README.md
      - AGENTS.md
    allowedFiles:
      - src/mcp-server.example.txt
      - README.md
    disallowedFiles:
      - .env
    expectedActions:
      - Preserve auth and privacy guidance.
    expectedOutputs:
      - Example invocation and expected output summary.
    successCriteria:
      - Example does not include secrets.
      - Example shows the required query parameter.
    verificationCommands:
      - npm test
    riskLevel: low
    commonFailureModes:
      - Uses a private-data tool for public docs.
      - Omits required schema fields.
textByPath.docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:
# Architecture

- `src/mcp-server.example.txt` contains static MCP tool examples.
- `agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml` defines verifiable agent workflows.
- `AGENTS.md` defines agent operating rules.

Tool metadata should explain intent, schemas, examples, auth/privacy requirements, and errors.
